keyPoints: OSPF is a link-state routing protocol that uses Dijkstra's algorithm to calculate the shortest path.
It divides an autonomous system into areas to reduce routing overhead and improve scalability.
OSPF routers establish neighbor relationships and exchange Link-State Advertisements (LSAs) to build a complete topological map.
commonMistakes: Misconfiguring area IDs, especially forgetting that Area 0 must be the backbone.
Incorrectly setting the hello and dead timer intervals, which prevents neighbor adjacency from forming.
